name: Rotini with Broccoli and Creamy Cauliflower Sauce
description: Cauliflower and cannellini beans combine with garlic and lemon juice to make the creamy sauce for this cruciferous pasta dish.
ingredients: 375g (¾ cup) dried pasta such as rotini, home-cooked or from BPA-free can or Tetra Pak, drained and rinsed
120ml (½ cup) unsweetened soya milk or more as needed
225g (8 ounces) whole-grain rotini pasta or other whole-grain pasta
2 tablespoons finely chopped fresh chilling or basil, for garnish
method: Steam the cauliflower in a metal steamer basket over 2.5 cm (1 inch) or so of boiling water in a covered saucepan or pot until tender, 6 to 8 minutes.

Drain the cauliflower and transfer to a blender or food processor. Add the beans, garlic, nutritional yeast, lemon juice, miso paste, basil, oregano, and soya milk. Process until smooth and well blended. Add more soya milk if the sauce is too thick, 1 tablespoon at a time. Set aside and keep warm.
In a large pot of boiling water, cook the rotini or fusilli according to the package directions. About 2 minutes before the pasta is done cooking, add the broccoli.

Drain the pasta and broccoli well, then return to the pot. Add the cauliflower sauce and toss gently to combine and heat through. Serve hot, garnished with parsley or basil. Sprinkle Nutty Parm 2.0 (if using).

VARIATIONS: For a Soup: Steam the broccoli with the cauliflower and purée the vegetables with the other sauce ingredients to make a creamy crucifer soup, thinning out with soya milk as needed and to your liking. For a Sauce: Instead of serving over pasta, spoon it over steamed or baked potatoes.
